pr.title: Patch postprocess.py to make lbcommand match
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Adjusted the ELF postprocess epilogue-detection logic so the C implementations of lbcommand timer commands could match, allowing inline assembly fallbacks for Command_04 and Command_08 to be removed. The PR also moved lbl_804D79E8 from the global asm/sdata2.s block into asm/melee/lb/lbcommand.s while removing lbl_804D79E0 from asm/sdata2.s, reflecting the new matching output's data needs.
what_changed: Removed NON_MATCHING guards and inline assembly implementations for Command_04 and Command_08 in src/melee/lb/lbcommand.c, leaving the plain C versions active.
Command_04 now relies on the C expression that adds the masked command data value, `*info->data_position & 0x3ffffff`, to `info->timer` and advances `data_position` by one word.
Command_08 now relies on the C expression that sets `info->timer` to the masked command data value minus `info->frame_count` and advances `data_position` by one word.
Updated tools/postprocess.py epilogue handling to treat `addi r1, r1, 0x18` (`0x38210018`) as a possible old-stack epilogue marker when no `mtlr_pos` has been found.
Relaxed the old epilogue distance assertion in postprocess.py from `<= 6 * 4` to `<= 7 * 4`, allowing one additional instruction word in the recognized epilogue window.
Moved the `lbl_804D79E8` sdata2 constant containing `0x7F7FFFFF, 0x00000000` from asm/sdata2.s into the lbcommand-specific assembly file asm/melee/lb/lbcommand.s.
Removed `lbl_804D79E0`, containing `0x43300000, 0x00000000`, from asm/sdata2.s in this diff.
smart_moves: Fixed the matching blocker in the project tooling instead of preserving local inline assembly in lbcommand.c, improving future decompilation leverage beyond a single function.
Kept the C implementations of Command_04 and Command_08 simple and readable once the postprocessing issue was addressed.
The postprocess.py change targets a specific observed epilogue shape, `addi r1, r1, 0x18` followed by `blr`, rather than broadly disabling or removing old-epilogue checks.
Relaxing the assertion by exactly one instruction word preserved a bounded safety check while accommodating the lbcommand output.
decomp_lessons: When a C function only fails to match because postprocessing does not recognize a compiler-emitted epilogue shape, consider extending postprocess.py before falling back to inline assembly.
Old-stack or epilogue patching logic may need to recognize stack-restoration instructions such as `addi r1, r1, 0x18` even when an explicit `mtlr` marker is absent.
A matching fix can require synchronized changes across C source, assembly data placement, and build/postprocess tooling.
Removing inline assembly after a tooling fix is valuable because it exposes the real C logic and reduces future maintenance burden.
Small changes to sdata2 labels can be a symptom of compiler/codegen differences caused by replacing inline assembly with matching C.
naming_conventions: Command handlers in lbcommand use `Command_XX` names, such as `Command_00`, `Command_04`, and `Command_08`, corresponding to command IDs.
The command context type is named `CommandInfo` and includes fields evidenced by this PR: `timer`, `frame_count`, and `data_position`.
Unidentified sdata2 labels retain generated names such as `lbl_804D79E8` and `lbl_804D79F0` when no semantic name is established.
postprocess.py helper variables use descriptive epilogue-scanning names such as `mtlr_pos`, `blr_pos`, and `epilogues`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Recognize PowerPC instruction word `0x38210018` as `addi r1, r1, 0x18` in postprocess.py when scanning for old epilogues.
Allow old epilogue patching to span up to seven instructions between the detected marker and `blr` instead of six.
For timer command matching, the C masked integer-to-float-style behavior from the removed assembly corresponds to masking the command word with `0x3ffffff` before updating `timer`.
The removed inline assembly used the standard `0x4330` double-conversion constant label `lbl_804D79E0`; after the tooling fix, that explicit assembly path was no longer needed in lbcommand.c.
Relocating sdata2 constants into the per-module assembly file can align the assembly data layout with the newly matching C object.
searchable_terms: Patch postprocess.py to make lbcommand match
follow_up_queries: Find other postprocess.py PRs that changed old epilogue or old stack patching logic.
Find other PRs that removed NON_MATCHING inline assembly after a tooling adjustment.
Search for remaining lbcommand Command_XX handlers that still rely on inline assembly or NON_MATCHING guards.
Search for sdata2 label relocations tied to matching C conversions or float/double constants.
